questions.explanation: The absence of the rule of law will bring about human rights abuse, as government can act arbitrarily without legal constraints or accountability.

questions.question: Centralization of power is the basic feature of
questions.explanation: Centralization of power is the basic feature of a unitary government, where all authority is concentrated in the national government.

questions.question: A law passed by the legislature and assented to by the executive is
questions.explanation: A law passed by the legislature and assented to by the executive is called an Act (or Act of Parliament).

questions.question: One of the features of a sovereign state is that it
options: has the capacity to defend itself from external aggression
has a large number of soldiers
practices the presidential system of government
is not indebted to other countries
questions.explanation: A sovereign state has the capacity to defend itself from external aggression, exercising supreme authority over its territory and independence from external control.

questions.question: A major way of maintaining confidence in the electoral process is by ensuring that
options: elections are conducted in a free and fair atmosphere
elections are conducted as and when due
questions.explanation: Maintaining confidence in the electoral process requires ensuring that elections are conducted in a free and fair atmosphere, with impartial administration and genuine competition.

questions.question: The principle of checks and balances reinforces separation of power in order to
options: protect the powers of the executive
prevent an unconstitutional change of government
questions.explanation: Checks and balances reinforce separation of powers to prevent the emergence of dictatorship by allowing each branch to limit the powers of the others.

questions.question: Legislative control of public corporation in Nigeria is effected through
questions.explanation: Legislative control of public corporations in Nigeria is effected through acts of the National Assembly, which establish, regulate, and oversee these corporations.

questions.question: The Ibadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provision of the
questions.explanation: The Ibadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provisions of the Macpherson Constitution, leading to its adoption in 1951.

questions.question: The fees collected by local governments at motor parks represent
questions.explanation: Fees collected at motor parks represent user charges (or service charges), paid by users for services provided (parking facilities, security, etc.).

questions.question: The first Nigerian constitution to provide for the fundamental objectives and directive principles of state policy was the
questions.explanation: The 1979 Constitution was the first Nigerian constitution to include the Fundamental Objectives and Directive Principles of State Policy in Chapter II.

questions.question: Under the 1999 Constitution of Nigeria, the power to create local government is vested in the
questions.explanation: Under the 1999 Constitution, the power to create local government is vested in the National Assembly, acting on state assembly resolutions.

questions.question: The body set up to review the revenue allocation formula in 1980 was the
questions.explanation: The Okigbo Commission (Presidential Commission on Revenue Allocation) was set up in 1980 to review the revenue allocation formula in Nigeria.

questions.question: In pre-colonial Igbo society, decisions on public issues were reached mainly through
questions.explanation: In pre-colonial Igbo society, decisions on public issues were reached mainly through consensus in village assemblies (Oha na Eze), seeking general agreement rather than majority vote.

questions.question: The main duty of the Local Government Service Commission is to
options: supervise and manage the affairs of the local governments
create job opportunities at the local level
handle request for the creation of more local governments
questions.explanation: The main duty of the Local Government Service Commission is to supervise and manage the affairs of local governments, including personnel matters and administration.

questions.question: The constitution that introduced restricted franchise into Nigeria politics was the
questions.explanation: The Clifford Constitution (1922) introduced restricted franchise (based on income and gender) into Nigerian politics, allowing only male adults with £100 annual income to vote.

questions.question: In Nigeria, the Council of State was first created by
questions.explanation: The Council of State was first created by Major-General Aguiyi-Ironsi in 1966 as an advisory body, though its composition and role have evolved over time.

questions.question: The leading agent in the expansion of British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s was the
questions.explanation: The Royal Niger Company (chartered company) was the leading agent in expanding British influence in Northern Nigeria in the 1890s, through treaties and trade.

questions.question: The two military coups that toppled Civilian regimes in Nigeria were those of
questions.explanation: The two military coups that toppled civilian regimes in Nigeria were January 15, 1966 (First Republic) and December 31, 1983 (Second Republic).

questions.question: The main function of the Code of Conduct Bureau is to
options: give the judiciary more power to discipline erring judges
protect public officers from the press
give the police more powers to make arrests
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau ensures accountability in government business by monitoring public officers' assets and enforcing the Code of Conduct for public officers.

questions.question: The emirate system of administration can be likened to a
questions.explanation: The emirate system of administration can be likened to a unitary system of government, with centralized authority in the Emir and a hierarchical administrative structure.

questions.question: The abolition of the state ministries of local government in 1989 entails that local governments
options: have more control over their funds
no longer have anything to do with state governments
are no longer subordinate to state governments
questions.explanation: The abolition of state ministries of local government in 1989 meant local governments were no longer subordinate to state governments, gaining direct federal funding and autonomy.

questions.question: The main legislative body in Nigeria between 1966 and 1975 was the
questions.explanation: The main legislative body in Nigeria between 1966 and 1975 (Gowon era) was the Supreme Military Council (SMC), which issued decrees as laws.

questions.question: Nigeria formally became a federation in
questions.explanation: Nigeria formally became a federation in 1914 with the amalgamation of the Northern and Southern Protectorates, though federal structures were developed later.

questions.question: The United Nations charter is an instrument that
options: sets out the rights and obligations of member states
provides for funding of member states
facilitates the resolution of disputes among member states
determines the admission of member states
questions.explanation: The UN Charter is the founding instrument that sets out the rights and obligations of member states, establishing the organization's principles and structures.

questions.question: The most active organ of the Economic Community of West African states is the
options: Authority of Heads of States and Government
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers is the most active organ of ECOWAS, meeting regularly to implement policies and oversee the community's operations.

questions.question: The group of states that conceived the idea of the United Nation Organization included
options: the USSR, Italy, the UK and China
Italy, the UK, France and Japan
the USA, the USSR, the UK and China
France, the USA, Canada and Japan
questions.explanation: The idea of the UN was conceived by the Allied powers in WWII: the USA, USSR, UK, and China (the 'Big Four').

questions.question: The action of the Commonwealth of Nation's is felt most in the area of
questions.explanation: The Commonwealth's action is felt most in the area of economic cooperation, including trade, development assistance, and technical cooperation among member states.

questions.question: The greatest criticism of the Security Council of the UNO is that
questions.explanation: The greatest criticism of the Security Council is that it is not representative enough, with permanent membership limited to five powers and excluding developing regions like Africa and Latin America.

questions.question: Which of the following is a founding member of OPEC?
questions.explanation: Venezuela is a founding member of OPEC (1960), along with Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, and Saudi Arabia. Nigeria joined later in 1971.

questions.question: Professor Ibrahim Gambari is the Special Assistant to the United Nation Secretary General on
questions.explanation: Professor Ibrahim Gambari served as the Special Assistant to the UN Secretary-General on African affairs.

questions.question: Who among the following served as Secretary General of OPEC?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rilwanu Lukman (Nigeria) served as Secretary-General of OPEC from 1995 to 2000, and also served as President of OPEC multiple times.

questions.question: Nigeria's active role in the liberation of some countries in Southern Africa earned her
options: the status of the frontline state
questions.explanation: Nigeria's active role in Southern African liberation struggles earned her the status of a Frontline State, supporting independence movements against apartheid and colonialism.

questions.question: Which of these international agencies is Nigeria a member of?
questions.explanation: Nigeria is a member of the International Monetary Fund (IMF), having joined in 1961. The Paris Club and London Club are creditor groups.

questions.question: Nigeria's membership of the ECOWAS is informed by her desire to
options: develop a market in the sub-region
questions.explanation: Nigeria's membership of ECOWAS is driven by her desire to promote economic integration in West Africa through trade liberalization and cooperation.

questions.question: An attribute that Nigeria shares with most non-aligned countries is
questions.explanation: An attribute Nigeria shares with most non-aligned countries is the state of her economy - they are mostly developing countries with similar economic challenges.
